crates/chemsema-cli/src/agent/capture.rs.reason: One exhaustive command boundary validates capture options, chooses the requested encoder, and emits one protocol response. Splitting the branches would duplicate output and error semantics.

crates/chemsema-cli/src/agent/context.rs.reason: One exhaustive context-query boundary keeps selector validation and response shaping atomic.

crates/chemsema-cli/src/main.rs:$file.reason: The file is the composition root for the independently implemented agent modules; production logic is reviewed separately by function.

crates/chemsema-cli/src/main.rs:run_comm.reason: The function owns one transaction lifecycle: load, execute, scope-check, report, and persist.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/cdxml.rs:pars.reason: One ordered document parse pipeline preserves table, page, object, normalization, and verification precedence.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/cdxml/export..reason: The exhaustive line-object writer keeps mutually dependent ArrowType and endpoint fields in one precedence table.
The exhaustive graphic writer maps one normalized shape payload to the official Graphic attributes.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/cdxml/import_.reason: The function resolves the interdependent ArrowType, endpoint, curve, and style fields as one official-field rule.
Bracket geometry, attachments, repeat semantics, and linked labels are one source-order-sensitive import transaction.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/engine/comman.reason: Immediate and creation command families are already separated; the remaining body is an exhaustive variant-to-domain dispatch with shared completion semantics.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/engine/chemis.reason: Sanitization, layout, stereochemical projection, object creation, and selection form one undoable insertion transaction.
The function creates one chemistry graph while preserving target filtering, stereochemistry, and source authority.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/engine/contex.reason: One ordered schema builder applies selection precedence and exposes mutually exclusive semantic commands.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/engine/text_e.reason: The module is the single authority for label recognition, layout, glyph geometry, and attachment refresh.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/legacy_mol.rs.reason: One record parser preserves V2000 atom, bond, property, charge, isotope, and alias ordering.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/render_object.reason: The function renders one ordered atom-property layer so charge, radical, query, map, and diagnostic placement share the same anchor.
Orbital template, phase, fill, outline, and rotation are one geometry rule with shared axes.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/render_svg.rs.reason: One exhaustive RenderPrimitive serializer guarantees identical role, identity, transform, and style emission.

crates/chemsema-engine/src/render/labels.reason: The function applies one ordered geometry pipeline for endpoint retreat, contact joins, crossings, and primitive emission.

apps/chemsema-office/src/windows_office/.reason: The function is one ordered bond-style projection table whose branches share endpoint, width, and presentation-mode precedence.
One exhaustive primitive dispatch keeps the Office vector surface's transforms, clipping, and style precedence consistent.

viewer/editor_overlay.js:renderEditorOve.reason: One ordered overlay layer prevents selection, focus, hover, preview, and text affordances from changing z-order.

viewer/editor_pointer_controller.js:hand.reason: One gesture state-machine entry preserves capture and precedence across selection, arrow, shape, text, and creation tools.
Geometry and move commits are already separate; this function retains the ordered state-machine transition and shared cleanup.

viewer/primitive_dom_renderer.js:renderC.reason: One exhaustive primitive dispatch keeps identity attributes, roles, transforms, and SVG z-order consistent.
